title: Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors
subtitle: Net [official development assistance](#dod:oda) to medical research and basic health sectors. This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors, 2023

Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors. This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.

The choropleth map displays the geographic distribution of net official development assistance directed toward medical research and basic health sectors for the year 2023, with values expressed in constant 2022 US dollars. The visualization uses a sequential color scale ranging from pale yellow for the lowest assistance amounts to deep navy blue for the highest, covering a spectrum from zero to over 300 million dollars. The highest concentrations of funding, represented by the darkest blue shades indicating values exceeding 300 million dollars, are visible across a cluster of nations in East and Central Africa, specifically Ethiopia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Tanzania. India also appears in this highest bracket, receiving substantial assistance relative to other regions. Moving down the scale, countries such as Nigeria, Kenya, Uganda, and Mozambique fall into the mid-to-high range, shaded in medium blues that correspond to assistance levels between 100 million and 300 million dollars. In South America, Brazil and Colombia occupy the lower-middle bands, receiving between 30 million and 100 million dollars. Conversely, vast areas of the map show minimal activity; large portions of South America, including Argentina and Chile, along with countries in North Africa and the Middle East like Egypt and Saudi Arabia, are colored in pale yellow or light green, signifying assistance levels below 30 million dollars or near zero. Significant regions, including most of North America, Western Europe, Australia, and Russia, are hatched in grey, denoting that no data is reported for these areas as they are typically donors rather than recipients in this dataset. The data shows a distinct concentration of high-value health sector assistance in specific sub-Saharan African nations and India, while the rest of the developing world receives comparatively lower amounts or falls outside the reporting scope.
